ordinary_no_progress_guard.fingerprint: ordinary|**read-only ux brief** **aesthetic direction:** refined institutional workbench. manus-inspired structure, but innomcp-native tone: light, thai-first, precise, calm teal/green signals, sarabun body + chakra petch display, no neon/dark/purple-gradient treatment. **framework:** next.js 16 + react 19 + tailwind 4. **current anchors** - `chatpage.tsx` already owns `chatmode`, websocket answer stream, sse agent stream, sticky composer, inline panel, and `xl` right rail. - `useagenteventstream.ts` already exposes public-safe `agentevent`, `drafttext`, `finaltext`, `status`, `warnings`, and forbidden-key filtering. - `multiagentpanel.tsx` already groups events per agent, but it is mostly a collapsible summary, not yet a true clickable log/workbench. - `chatmessage.tsx` already places `inlineextras` above the answer, which is the right boundary for “thinking strip first, answer remains primary.” - `thinkingpanel.tsx` is older/public-safe transcript ui; avoid duplicating it into the main chat unless preserving living-chat routes requires it. **component boundaries** - `chatpage.tsx`: orchestration only. add state such as `selectedworkbenchagentid`, `workbenchopen`, and pass `chatmode`, `selectedagentid`, `onselectagent`, `variant`. - `useagenteventstream.ts`: keep as the event source. add derived helpers/types only if needed, not ui logic. - `multiagentpanel.tsx`: split internally into reusable pieces: - `agentrunheader` - `agentworklist` - `agentworkitem` - `agentlogdrawer` or detail pane - `workbenchpanel` - `chatmessage.tsx`: keep answer rendering. it should receive a compact inline work strip only; no right-rail concerns. - `thinkingpanel.tsx`: either leave for `/living-chat` or gradually replace with the same public-safe work item renderer to avoid two log uis. **exact data model** use existing `agentevent` as the source: ```ts type agentworkitem = { id: string; // `${runid}:${agentid ?? "run"}:${type}:${timestamp}:${index}` runid: string; messageid: string; agentid: string; role?: string; type: agenteventtype; status: "queued" | "active" | "done" | "recovering" | "error"; title: string; // thai label from type/agent role summary: string; // publicsummary only timestamp: string; model?: string; provider?: string; toolname?: string; confidence?: number; sourceids?: string[]; latencyms?: number; isclickable: boolean; }; ``` do not introduce private reasoning fields. never render `privatethought`, `hiddenreasoning`, `chainofthought`, `rawthought`, `innermonologue`, secrets, api keys, or raw payload dumps. **mode behavior** - normal mode: - backend contract stays `preferredmode: "remote"`, `reasoningmode: "normal"`. - show one progressive answer bubble. - show a collapsed inline “work tape” above the answer only when sse events exist. - no right-side workbench by default. - keep the agent count visually restrained; memory says normal mode should preserve the two-agent contract. - thinking mode / current `multiagent`: - backend contract stays `preferredmode: "hybrid"`, `reasoningmode: "thinking"`. - still one progressive answer, never multiple competing answer bubbles. - inline strip stays collapsed by default above the answer. - right-side live workbench appears on `xl+`, open by default, showing active agents, tools, status, timing, and selected log details. - thinking mode may show more than two agents. **clickable log behavior** - every agent row/work item is a real `<button>` with `aria-expanded` or `aria-selected`. - click agent row in inline strip: opens/focuses right workbench on `xl+`; on smaller screens opens an inline drawer below the strip. - click a work item: reveals public-safe event detail: thai status label, `publicsummary`, `toolname`, `model`, `provider`, `confidence`, timestamp, sources. - keyboard: - `enter`/`space` opens selected item. - `escape` closes mobile drawer. - existing `ctrl+o` can expand/collapse all, but `multiagentpanel` should actually consume the passed `ontoggleexpandall` or remove that prop. - no raw event json in the ui. **responsive behavior** - `< xl`: no right rail. workbench becomes an inline drawer inside the latest assistant bubble, below the compact strip and above source chips/answer text. - `xl+`: two-column document flow: main answer max width remains around `50rem`; right workbench sticky at `top-20`, width `18rem-22rem`. - normal mode: no empty right rail. - thinking mode: workbench visible even before agents start, using dormant/warming states. - composer stays sticky and must not overlap the last answer; preserve current natural document scroll. **acceptance tests** - add/update playwright around existing `multiagent-panel.spec.ts` and `acceptance.spec.ts`. - normal mode: - `inno-workspace-sidebar` is not attached. - sending a message renders exactly one latest assistant answer surface. - inline work strip may appear, collapsed, above answer text. - thinking mode: - clicking `chat-mode-multiagent` attaches `inno-workspace-sidebar` at `1400x900`. - sending a prompt reaches `multiagent-expand-all` or equivalent active workbench selector. - clicking an agent row opens public-safe log detail. - security: - dom and `/api/chat/stream` response text contain no forbidden private reasoning keys. - responsive: - at mobile/tablet widths, sidebar is absent and clicking the inline strip opens an inline/mobile drawer. - regression: - final answer remains monotonic: sse preview/final cannot replace a longer websocket structured answer. - weather/chart structured content is not overwritten by mdes final text. stop condition for executor: code compiles, targeted playwright tests pass, and screenshots show one progressive answer plus clickable public-safe logs without overpowering the answer.

ordinary|## bug report **symptom**: a1/a2/s1 dispatch artifacts repeatedly contain only `exception of type 'system.outofmemoryexception' was thrown.` **root cause**: the mdes delegate is trying to append to `docs/reports/skill-usage-log.md`, but that log is corrupted/oversized: `get-item` reports `10.29gb`, and a read-only `get-content` measurement itself reproduced `system.outofmemoryexception`. the helper reads the entire log into memory before every append at [delegate-to-mdes.ps1:86](</c:/users/user-nt/dev/innomcp/scripts/delegate-to-mdes.ps1:86>) and rewrites it at [delegate-to-mdes.ps1:107](</c:/users/user-nt/dev/innomcp/scripts/delegate-to-mdes.ps1:107>). the loop calls the helper without disabling logging at [innomcp-mdes-loop.ps1:102](</c:/users/user-nt/dev/innova-bot-template/scripts/innomcp-mdes-loop.ps1:102>). **reproduction**: read-only repro was `get-content c:\users\user-nt\dev\innomcp\docs\reports\skill-usage-log.md`; it throws `system.outofmemoryexception`. recent artifacts corroborate the repeated dispatch failure, e.g. [summary.md:12](</c:/users/user-nt/dev/innova-bot-template/docs/innomcp-mdes-loop/loop-20260520-140736/summary.md:12>) through [summary.md:14](</c:/users/user-nt/dev/innova-bot-template/docs/innomcp-mdes-loop/loop-20260520-140736/summary.md:14>). **ranked causes**: 1. **high confidence**: oversized/corrupted `skill-usage-log.md` plus whole-file read/write logging. this is the only cause i directly reproduced locally. 2. **medium confidence**: earlier request shape could have contributed: uncapped, non-streaming dispatches and heavier model choices. current uncommitted changes now add prompt/output caps at [delegate-to-mdes.ps1:180](</c:/users/user-nt/dev/innomcp/scripts/delegate-to-mdes.ps1:180>) and [delegate-to-mdes.ps1:186](</c:/users/user-nt/dev/innomcp/scripts/delegate-to-mdes.ps1:186>), and a1/a2 are now `gemma4:e4b` at [innomcp-mdes-loop.ps1:78](</c:/users/user-nt/dev/innova-bot-template/scripts/innomcp-mdes-loop.ps1:78>) and [innomcp-mdes-loop.ps1:79](</c:/users/user-nt/dev/innova-bot-template/scripts/innomcp-mdes-loop.ps1:79>). 3. **lower confidence / amplifier**: the loop buffers full child output through `out-string` at [innomcp-mdes-loop.ps1:103](</c:/users/user-nt/dev/innova-bot-template/scripts/innomcp-mdes-loop.ps1:103>), and watch mode repeats failures every tick at [innomcp-mdes-loop.ps1:295](</c:/users/user-nt/dev/innova-bot-template/scripts/innomcp-mdes-loop.ps1:295>). **smallest safe fix plan**: 1. for this loop, pass `-log $false` at [innomcp-mdes-loop.ps1:102](</c:/users/user-nt/dev/innova-bot-template/scripts/innomcp-mdes-loop.ps1:102>) so dispatch no longer touches the 10gb log. 2. in the helper, replace whole-file log insertion with bounded append or skip logging when the file exceeds a small cap, around [delegate-to-mdes.ps1:69](</c:/users/user-nt/dev/innomcp/scripts/delegate-to-mdes.ps1:69>). 3. quarantine/rotate the corrupted `skill-usage-log.md` before re-enabling helper logging. i did not edit or revert anything. similar pattern check found only this mdes helper doing the `skill-usage-log.md` write path; `out-string` buffering also exists in the loop but is secondary until the log issue is removed.

ordinary|use a **mobile “agent activity sheet”**: a compact bottom sheet that only exists below `xl`, while desktop keeps the current right rail unchanged. **trigger:** show a small sticky pill above the composer when a run is idle/active: `mdes activity · 2 active` with a status dot and chevron. it should also appear in normal mode, because normal mode still has the mdes 2-agent contract. **content:** first screen should be dense and safe: current phase, active agents, tool calls, public summaries, artifacts count, and final status. use thai-first labels like `บันทึกการทำงาน`, `เครื่องมือที่ใช้`, `แหล่งข้อมูล`, `คำตอบพร้อมแล้ว`. keep logs to approved `publicsummary` style text only. **close behavior:** swipe down, esc, backdrop tap, and a visible close button. persist closed state only for the current response; reopen automatically for the next new run if activity starts. **avoid:** no raw reasoning, no “thinking transcript”, no manus-style workspace cloning, no full-screen takeover, no purple/glow/consumer-chat treatment, and no hiding the main answer behind the sheet. the sheet supports trust; the answer remains primary.

ordinary|read-only only. i inspected the three requested files and did not run tests. **findings** - **p1: workbench tests conflict with the new collapsed-default ux.** [chatpage.tsx](</c:/users/user-nt/dev/innomcp/innomcp-next/src/app/components/chat/chatpage.tsx:310>) defaults `desktopworkbenchopen` to `false`, and the desktop panel only renders when that state is true at [chatpage.tsx](</c:/users/user-nt/dev/innomcp/innomcp-next/src/app/components/chat/chatpage.tsx:1877>). but [multiagent-panel.spec.ts](</c:/users/user-nt/dev/innomcp/innomcp-next/e2e/multiagent-panel.spec.ts:18>) and [acceptance.spec.ts](</c:/users/user-nt/dev/innomcp/innomcp-next/e2e/acceptance.spec.ts:1303>) still expect `[data-testid="agent-workbench-root"]` to be visible without opening the toggle. - **p1: the “one progressive assistant message” contract is not protected.** the ws path can create a progress ai message at [chatpage.tsx](</c:/users/user-nt/dev/innomcp/innomcp-next/src/app/components/chat/chatpage.tsx:696>) and then append a real ai message when the last one is progress at [chatpage.tsx](</c:/users/user-nt/dev/innomcp/innomcp-next/src/app/components/chat/chatpage.tsx:656>). the mdes draft/final merge updates the last non-progress ai message at [chatpage.tsx](</c:/users/user-nt/dev/innomcp/innomcp-next/src/app/components/chat/chatpage.tsx:950>), but tests mostly read only the last assistant text via [acceptance.spec.ts](</c:/users/user-nt/dev/innomcp/innomcp-next/e2e/acceptance.spec.ts:111>), so duplicate assistant bubbles or leaked progress text could pass. - **p2: localstorage can hide collapsed-default regressions.** the workbench open state is restored from `innomcp.desktopworkbenchopen` at [chatpage.tsx](</c:/users/user-nt/dev/innomcp/innomcp-next/src/app/components/chat/chatpage.tsx:370>). in [multiagent-panel.spec.ts](</c:/users/user-nt/dev/innomcp/innomcp-next/e2e/multiagent-panel.spec.ts:13>), localstorage is cleared after `goto`, which is too late to guarantee first-render collapsed state. - **p2: the collapsed desktop toggle may still read too loud.** the fixed right toggle includes label, status summary, and idle agent count at [chatpage.tsx](</c:/users/user-nt/dev/innomcp/innomcp-next/src/app/components/chat/chatpage.tsx:1517>). that risks feeling like a secondary dashboard rather than a quiet inspectable workbench. - empty desktop home: no right-rail panel, no feature/trust strip, prompt suggestions stay capped and compact, with the composer as the dominant element.
